cold_open.hook: FDA commissioner Marty Makary just resigned after the White House forced him to approve fruity e-cigs—regulatory capture, but make it *flavored*.

markets.macro_note: Small caps getting hammered (-0.97% Russell), VIX cooling, but chip stocks rolling over—the reversal pattern is real.

big_story.story_title: Chip stocks suffer rare selloff on bearish 'key reversal' chart pattern
big_story.angle: This isn't just technical noise—it's the first real crack in the AI-everything narrative; what's actually broken under the hood of semiconductor demand?

quick_hits.angle: Companies are getting tariff refunds after the Supreme Court decision—this is the market finally pricing in that Trump's trade war has an expiration date.
Waymo recalled 3,800 robotaxis because they drove into standing water—the future of autonomous vehicles is apparently 'don't go outside when it rains.'
Nonprofits are in crisis mode—turns out relying on billionaire whims and grant cycles is not a sustainable business model; who knew.
Anthropic is warning investors away from secondary share platforms—the company that lectures everyone about AI safety is choosing violence against its own cap table.

odd_thing.joke_angle: Americans' views on China are shifting—turns out geopolitical tension is a better brand repositioner than any Super Bowl ad.

yesterday_callback.topic: Kevin Hassett says AI isn't costing jobs right now—meanwhile tech companies have laid off 262,000 people since 2022.
yesterday_callback.fresh_take: Waymo's water-phobic robotaxis just recalled 3,800 units—so AI is simultaneously not killing jobs AND apparently can't handle a puddle; the narrative is collapsing faster than the chip chart.

sign_off.callback_target: Waymo's standing water problem—close the show with 'at least the robotaxis know their limits, unlike the Fed.'
